description: This is a minimalistic implementation of visualization options for Conos. This module is intended to be used after Conos.Cluster has been ran.
author: Edwin F. Juarez;UCSD - Mesirov Lab
plan: ## Conos.VisualizeGeneExpression GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan ### 1. Overview Conos.VisualizeGeneExpression is a GenePattern module that This is a minimalistic implementation of visualization options for Conos. This module is intended to be used after Conos.Cluster has been ran. ### 2. Parameters The module exposes 4 parameters: - Required: conos_object, genesofinterest, clustering, output_file_name - Optional: none ### 3. Command Line ``` Rscript --no-save --quiet --slave --no-restore run_conos_visualizegeneexpression.R --conos_object --genesofinterest --clustering --output_file_name ``` ### 4. Docker Image Docker image: genepattern/conos\:2.1 ### 5. Validation Strategy - Validate all required parameters are provided - Validate file formats match expected extensions - Check parameter value ranges where applicable ### 6. Implementation Roadmap Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation Phase 2: Docker container setup Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: genesofinterest
parameters.description: A list of gene identifiers. The expression of these genes will be plotted on the embedded map. Gene names need to be separated by a coma and a space (e.g. MS4A1, CD74, LYZ, CD14) and match the identifiers used in the original input data (Gene Symbols, Ensembl IDs, etc).

parameters.name: clustering
parameters.description: Option to show clustering information besides gene expression (none, leiden, walktrap, both)

parameters.name: output_file_name
parameters.description: Basename of the file to be saved
